raw.objective: “to assess LEV PK in a pediatric population using a population approach to identify demographic and physiologic determinants of LEV concentration, including the effects of concomitant AEDs. The optimal pediatric dosing regimens were also defined to achieve plasma concentrations within a given target range.”
raw.covariates: Tested: time after dose, gender, age, WT, serum creatinine, comedication, creatinine clearance. Included: WT on CL and Vd
raw.observations: A lag in absorption of circa 15 minutes was observed. The VPC shows that the model does not fully capture the absorption kinetics, with the prediction intervals not including many peak concentrations. Otherwise it seems a decent fit for the amount of covariates used.
raw.structure: 1 compartment with linear absorption and elimination
raw.parameter_values: theta1=2.47 theta2=0.89 theta3=21.9 theta4=0.93 theta5=3.83 theta6=0.283 omega1=0.059 omega1,2=0.028 omega2=0.0265 omega3=1.37 sigma1=0.0357

structural_model.description: 1 compartment with linear absorption and elimination

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

omega_covariance.conversion: Appendix covariance retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to assess LEV PK in a pediatric population using a population approach to identify demographic and physiologic determinants of LEV concentration, including the effects of concomitant AEDs. The optimal pediatric dosing regimens were also defined to achieve plasma concentrations within a given target range.”

covariates.raw: Tested: time after dose, gender, age, WT, serum creatinine, comedication, creatinine clearance. Included: WT on CL and Vd

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
